User story: As any user, I can navigate between the LoanFlow app, AI-DLC artifacts, feature tracker, and README demo page from the top nav.
Preconditions: Demo database has users; app is running.
Expected behavior based on code: Header renders LoanFlow brand, LoanFlow App link to /, AI-DLC artifacts link to /aidlc, Feature tracker link to /feature-tracker, README link to /readme, plus Viewing as selector.
Test result: Passed by npm run story:check (48/48) plus targeted browser checks for nav, role switching, loan detail tabs, README, AI-DLC, feature tracker, and new-loan form.
Error or UX issue: Fixed: primary nav was hidden below md viewport, making README and AI-DLC demo links inaccessible on mobile.
Fix status: Fixed in components/Header.tsx by making nav visible and wrapping on small viewports, then renamed the root link to LoanFlow App for clearer demos.
Retest result: Passed: desktop and 390px mobile browser snapshots expose LoanFlow App, AI-DLC artifacts, Feature tracker, and README; README click opens /readme.

Feature: Next 16 route and cookie API compatibility
User story: As a developer/demo operator, I can build and run the app on the upgraded Next runtime without page or API 500s.
Preconditions: Next 16.2.9 is installed; app uses App Router dynamic pages and route handlers.
Expected behavior based on code: Dynamic pages and API handlers await params; getCurrentUser awaits cookies(); build and dev routes render without sync dynamic API errors.
Test result: Passed: npm run build on Next 16.2.9; npm run story:check 44/44; mobile README browser click passed.
Error or UX issue: Fixed: Next 15+ requires async params and cookies, which initially caused type errors and rendered-route 500s on stale dev server.
Fix status: Fixed in dynamic pages, dynamic route handlers, and lib/auth.ts.
Retest result: Passed after clean .next, fresh dev server, smoke, story checks, build, and mobile browser check.

Feature: npm audit posture for Next bundled PostCSS advisory
User story: As a developer/demo operator, I can see the remaining dependency audit status and know whether a safe repository fix exists.
Preconditions: npm audit is run after upgrading Next to 16.2.9.
Expected behavior based on code: High Next advisories are removed by upgrading framework/runtime dependencies; residual advisory is tracked with current npm metadata and TODO status.
Test result: npm audit still reports 2 moderate items via Next bundled postcss even on next@16.2.9; npm fixAvailable suggests next@9.3.3, which is not a safe/applicable fix.
Error or UX issue: Residual upstream dependency-audit warning; no safe app-level patch identified from npm metadata.
Fix status: Partially mitigated by upgrading Next from 14.2.33 to 16.2.9; keep TODO to re-run audit when Next publishes/remaps a modern patched release.
Retest result: Build, smoke, and 44/44 story checks pass on Next 16.2.9 despite residual moderate audit item.
